text.title: backlog-grooming-roadmapping Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for backlog-grooming-roadmapping as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Agent-generated backlog items look certain and well-reasoned but are pure hypothesis.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: LLMs produce polished text that creates false confidence. Text quality does not equal idea quality. Always mark AI-generated items as hypothesis and require user validation before writing a spec.

failure-mode.description: Agent assigns 100% Confidence in RICE scores to almost all items.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Agents lack data to justify lower confidence, so they default to 100%. Require explicit justification for Confidence above 80% in agent-generated scores.

failure-mode.description: Agent assigns high RICE scores to technically interesting items regardless of business value.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Agents cannot distinguish strategic P0s from tactical P0s without explicit business context. Human must validate strategic feasibility before items move to todo/.

failure-mode.description: Agent adds items to backlog but never drops or archives old ones.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Agents reword and reorder but rarely delete. Backlogs grow unbounded without explicit cleanup instructions. Archive items older than 6 months with no progress to `.aidocs/backlog/.archive/`.
